channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Congress delays vote on Republican-backed ICE funding after GOP infighting
item.description: Republicans upended plans to pass a partisan bill to fund ICE after party infighting over unrelated issues snarled their progress.
item.content:encoded: Republicans upended plans to pass a partisan bill to fund ICE after party infighting over unrelated issues snarled their progress.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt

item.title: Jeff Flake, former Republican senator, on party pushing back against Trump's agenda
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Jeff Flake, a former Republican senator, about this week's GOP pushback against some of President Trump's priorities.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Jeff Flake, a former Republican senator, about this week's GOP pushback against some of President Trump's priorities.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Commission of Fine Arts gives Trump's arch final approval, despite public pushback
item.description: The Commission of Fine Arts has given a design for a massive arch in Washington, D.C., its final approval, even though it's missing some visual components.
item.content:encoded: The Commission of Fine Arts has given a design for a massive arch in Washington, D.C., its final approval, even though it's missing some visual components.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Jack Antonoff says 'this version of modernity is trash' in new Bleachers album
item.description: Jack Antonoff explores marriage, grief and the current moment in Bleachers' latest album, everyone for ten minutes.
item.content:encoded:
Jack Antonoff explores marriage, grief and the current moment in Bleachers' latest album, everyone for ten minutes.

(Image credit: Alex Lockett)
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Senate breaks for recess, delaying vote on ICE funding, DNC releases 'autopsy' of 2024 presidential election, a look at Trump's political -- and personal -- interest in Cuba.
item.content:encoded: Senate breaks for recess, delaying vote on ICE funding, DNC releases 'autopsy' of 2024 presidential election, a look at Trump's political -- and personal -- interest in Cuba.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Eli Lilly says its next gen obesity drug is more powerful than Zepbound and Wegovy
item.description: Eli Lilly announced clinical trial results for retatrutide, its next gen obesity drug. The drug helped study participants lose 70 lbs over 80 weeks, making it more powerful than Zepbound and Wegovy.
item.content:encoded: Eli Lilly announced clinical trial results for retatrutide, its next gen obesity drug. The drug helped study participants lose 70 lbs over 80 weeks, making it more powerful than Zepbound and Wegovy.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Lupkin

item.title: Can Texas' power grid handle the demands of data centers?
item.description: Data centers are creating enormous demand for new electricity in Texas. But can the grid actually meet it?
item.content:encoded: Data centers are creating enormous demand for new electricity in Texas. But can the grid actually meet it?
item.dc:creator: Cooper Katz McKim

item.title: SpaceX to launch Starship rocket Friday after delay
item.description: SpaceX scrubbed the twelfth launch of its newly redesigned Starship moments before liftoff Thursday. The company will try again Friday and it comes at a critical time for SpaceX, which is seeking to go public.
item.content:encoded: SpaceX scrubbed the twelfth launch of its newly redesigned Starship moments before liftoff Thursday. The company will try again Friday and it comes at a critical time for SpaceX, which is seeking to go public.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: CBS Radio signs off after decades on the air, ending historic chapter in broadcasting
item.description: CBS Radio signs off for the last time Friday night, after almost a century that helped define broadcast news.
item.content:encoded: CBS Radio signs off for the last time Friday night, after almost a century that helped define broadcast news.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Takeaways from the DNC's 'autopsy' of the 2024 presidential election
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with political strategist and Clinton White House counselor Paul Begala about the DNC's "autopsy" dissecting its defeat in the 2024 presidential election.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with political strategist and Clinton White House counselor Paul Begala about the DNC's "autopsy" dissecting its defeat in the 2024 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep
